risk_if_not_implemented: Without Highest Classification Level, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Asset Categorization
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to categorize Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS).
compensating_control_1.justification: Asset Categorization (AST-31) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Highest Classification Level (DCH-02.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Data applicability. Given the data-handling focus of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Data & Asset Classification
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to ensure data and assets are categorized in accordance with applicable statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements.
compensating_control_2.justification: Data & Asset Classification (DCH-02) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Highest Classification Level (DCH-02.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Data applicability. Given the data-handling focus of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
